source_input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

cluster_summary.label: Japandi dining with rattan pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, woven rattan pendant, light linen-upholstered chairs, ceramic tableware. Stage with a single textured centerpiece (stoneware vase), neutral place settings, and warm-toned pendant to create an intimate focal point. Keep surfaces decluttered and emphasize wood grain and woven textures.

cluster_summary.label: Low-seating Japanese tea nook
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Floor-level cushions and low table on layered natural-fibre rug, bamboo/blind screens and soft lantern lighting. Arrange poufs and floor cushions for casual seating, add braided straw floor pads and low ambient lamps to enhance cozy, contemplative mood. Use warm, dimmable light and sparse greenery for balance.

cluster_summary.label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral sofa on low wooden base, oversized low-profile coffee table, potted plants and minimal wall art. Stage with textured rugs (wool + tatami edge), mixed neutral cushions, sculptural ceramics and small bonsai or leafy plants to introduce organic color and scale. Emphasize horizontal lines and negative space.

vibe_report.summary: A warm, calm Japandi scheme tuned for a young family: restrained minimalism, warm neutral woods, tactile natural fibers, and layered soft lighting. Prioritize horizontal lines, low-profile furniture and uncluttered sightlines while inserting a small family work corner and a safe crib location in the bedroom. Emphasize honest materials and quiet styling over decorative excess.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutrals anchored by pale cream and soft taupe for walls (#EDE7DF), mid-tone oak and honey woods (#CBB698/#A88461) for primary furniture, muted stone-gray accents (#6D6A5F) for metal and ceramics, and a deep char accent (#2B2724) sparingly for contrast. This palette keeps the mood calm while allowing wood grain and woven textures to read on camera.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft, layered ambient with warm temperature (2700–3000K). Key moments use late-afternoon golden side light from windows/patio; fill with low-level uplighting or table lamps. Use dimmable pendants over dining and warm floor lamps near the seating/work corner to create cozy vignettes.
materials: Light- to mid-tone oak (tables, shelving, low sofa bases)
Woven rattan and seagrass (pendants, baskets, chair seats)
Linen and cotton upholstery (sofas, cushions, curtains)
Wool and natural fiber rugs (wool, jute, tatami-edge mats)
furniture_language: Low-profile seating with exposed wooden base
Rounded table edges and subtle radiused corners
Integrated storage with flat, handleless faces
Multipurpose pieces (bench that doubles as toy storage)
styling_rules: Keep surfaces mostly clear; 1–2 sculptural objects per sightline
Group smaller objects in odd numbers and on mixed textures (wood + ceramic)
Layer rugs to create nested zones (e.g., tatami edge under a wool rug)
Use plants sparingly to introduce organic color and vertical life
Keep textiles neutral with one warm accent tone repeated (honey or clay)
avoid: Overly ornate or heavily patterned fabrics
Tall, bulky sofas that break the low-horizontal Japandi proportion
Excessive decor clutter—no more than 3 visible tabletop items per surface

room_guidance.headline: Low, warm family living with a compact standing-desk work nook
room_guidance.guidance: Anchor the room with a low neutral sofa on a low oak base facing the patio. Place a low, wide coffee table and a textured wool/tatami layered rug. Near the window/door to the patio, create a compact standing-desk corner using a slim oak standing desk and an unobtrusive task lamp so it reads integrated but not dominant. Keep toy storage hidden inside a low bench. Use one large sculptural ceramic and one plant as focal accents.
must_include: slim oak standing desk with task lamp
one medium potted plant (e.g., ficus/ficus lyrata or rubber plant)
must_avoid: tall shelving that breaks low-line composition
cluttered children's toys in open view

room_guidance.headline: Serene mid-size bedroom with safe crib and soft work corner option
room_guidance.guidance: Place a low wooden bed centered on the longest wall with clear walkways (min 2.5 ft) on both sides. Position the crib on the adjacent short wall away from direct window drafts and not directly under blinds or cords. A minimal dresser and a small, movable standing-desk console (if user prefers work corner in bedroom) should remain secondary and compact. Use warm bedside lamps and a soft layered rug to create a cocooned feel.
must_include: simple wooden crib positioned away from direct window/door
soft wool rug large enough to extend beyond bed edges
optional small standing-desk console (folding or slim profile)
must_avoid: crib directly under windows or heavy curtains

room_guidance.headline: Intimate Japandi dining nook with rattan pendant
room_guidance.guidance: Use a round or oval oak dining table to emphasize softness and circulation in the small bay nook. Suspend a woven rattan pendant centered over the table; keep seating simple with linen-upholstered chairs. Minimal table setting: a single stoneware vase with a dried branch or single stem. Keep surfaces clear and emphasize wood grain and pendant as focal point.
must_include: woven rattan pendant centered above table
must_avoid: long rectangular table that crowds circulation
multiple pendants or overly bright fixture
busy table linens or colorful dishware

room_guidance.headline: Clean compact kitchen with calm finishes and visual continuity
room_guidance.guidance: Keep cabinetry matte, handleless or with subtle integrated pulls in warm wood or off-white. Stage minimal countertop composition: a wooden cutting board, one small ceramic utensil crock, and one plant near the pass-through to the dining area. Use warm under-cabinet lighting and avoid cluttered appliances on counters.
must_include: one wooden cutting board and stoneware crock
compact appliance group kept in cabinet
must_avoid: multiple small appliances on visible counters

room_guidance.headline: Covered patio extension as an outdoor lounging nook
room_guidance.guidance: Create a sheltered outdoor sitting area that reads like an extension of the living room: low teak bench or lounge chairs, woven floor cushions, and a small side table. Use soft lantern lighting and a single large potted plant to frame the opening. Keep materials weathered/matte and textiles removable for outdoor use.
must_include: low teak or oak outdoor bench/chairs
soft lantern or wall-mounted warm light
must_avoid: indoor upholstery that looks delicate/unsuitable for outdoors

room_guidance.headline: Small, spa-like bathroom with tactile finishes
room_guidance.guidance: Use matte stone or tile in warm neutral tone. Keep vanity compact and wall-mounted if possible. Stage with folded neutral towels, a single small bonsai or succulent, and matte ceramic soap dispenser. Use warm downlights and a soft mirror light for flattering, even illumination.

room_guidance.headline: Minimal entry with discreet storage and a calming welcome
room_guidance.guidance: Design a small, tidy entry: low bench with shoe storage, a slim wall shelf or peg rail, and a woven basket for miscellaneous items. Keep mirror minimal and place a single sculptural object or small plant to avoid clutter. Ensure circulation into the living room is unobstructed.
must_include: low bench with hidden shoe storage
small mirror or sculptural wall object

warnings: Exact window positions and some room dimensions are inferred from the provided floor plan image. Verify final furniture clearance against measured site dimensions before procurement or staging.
Crib placement must comply with local safety codes — avoid placing crib directly under windows, cords, or blinds.
Kitchen and several ancillary rooms lack precise measurements; keep fixtures and staged elements compact and modular to avoid scale issues.

creative_spec.overall_mood: Calm, warm Japandi minimalism tailored for a young family — tactile, low, and uncluttered.
global_style_notes: Prioritize low, horizontal furniture silhouettes to maintain the Japandi proportion in camera.
Limit color accents to one warm clay/honey tone repeated across 2–3 elements (textile, small ceramic, plant pot).
Keep plantings medium-sized and sculptural rather than dense foliage beds.
Use layered rugs to define zones in the open plan without adding visual clutter.
Lighting should read warm and dimmable in footage; avoid harsh direct light on faces.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: No bright saturated colors, no high-gloss chrome, no ornate or baroque furniture, no cluttered tabletops, no visible safety hazards around crib (no cords/blinds/unstable shelving), avoid glossy reflections and overly staged accessory sets.

caption_context.property_brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.
caption_context.aesthetic_summary: A warm, calm Japandi scheme tuned for a young family: restrained minimalism, warm neutral woods, tactile natural fibers, and layered soft lighting. Prioritize horizontal lines, low-profile furniture and uncluttered sightlines while inserting a small family work corner and a safe crib location in the bedroom. Emphasize honest materials and quiet styling over decorative excess.

annotations.label: Mood
annotations.value: Calm, warm Japandi minimalism tailored for a young family — tactile, low, and uncluttered.

warnings.message: Several rooms lack precise measured dimensions; use compact, modular furniture and verify clearance in-situ before final staging.
Crib safety: avoid placing crib under windows, blinds, cords, or heavy shelving. Confirm final placement with owner and comply with crib safety guidelines.
Light and exposure recommendations assume late-afternoon shoot for warm tones; adjust color temperature if filming in different daylight conditions.
Exact window positions and some room dimensions are inferred from the provided floor plan image. Verify final furniture clearance against measured site dimensions before procurement or staging.
Crib placement must comply with local safety codes — avoid placing crib directly under windows, cords, or blinds.
Kitchen and several ancillary rooms lack precise measurements; keep fixtures and staged elements compact and modular to avoid scale issues.
